message:
feat(theme): Implement TuiStyles abstraction and integrate theming into session picker

Theme infrastructure (src/theme.rs):

- Add TuiStyles struct containing pre-built lipgloss styles derived from theme
  colors: title, muted, muted_bold, muted_italic, accent, accent_bold,
  success_bold, warning, warning_bold, error_bold, border, selection

- Add Theme::resolve() that loads the active theme from config with fallbacks:
  unset/empty → dark, "dark"/"light" → builtins, otherwise load by name

- Add Theme::is_light() using relative luminance calculation on background
  color to detect light themes for glamour style selection

- Add Theme::tui_styles() factory for creating TuiStyles from theme colors

- Add Theme::glamour_style_config() that returns a GlamourStyleConfig with
  document foreground, heading/link accent colors, and code styling applied

- Add parse_hex_color() helper for RGB extraction from #RRGGBB strings

Session picker theming (src/session_picker.rs):

- Replace hardcoded lipgloss styles (Style::new().foreground("212")) with
  TuiStyles fields derived from the active theme

- Add SessionPicker::with_theme() constructor that accepts a Theme and
  generates styles, keeping new() as a dark-theme default for tests

- Update pick_session() to load config and resolve theme before creating
  the picker, enabling user theme preferences to apply to session selection

- Apply styles.title for header, styles.muted/muted_bold for column headers
  and help text, styles.selection for highlighted row

This establishes the theme infrastructure for the TUI while deferring
runtime theme switching (tracked in bd-22p).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
